project.name: SWU OSR Platform
project.description: Campus open-source repository platform for STMIK Widya Utama students. Links SIAKAD academic identities with GitHub accounts, aggregates GitHub activity via webhooks, and provides pseudonymous profiles, repo showcases, a points-based leaderboard, and an internal discussion forum. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand SWU OSR: a campus open-source platform linking SIAKAD academic identities with GitHub, with showcases, activity feed, leaderboard, and forum.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: main.go wires configuration, PostgreSQL and Redis pools, repositories, services, handlers, middleware, and starts the Chi HTTP server — the composition root of the clean architecture.

tour.title: Domain Core
tour.description: The domain layer defines entities (User, ShowcaseRepo, ActivityLog, Thread) and repository/service interfaces that the rest of the backend depends on, keeping business rules independent of frameworks.

tour.title: Authentication & Identity Binding
tour.description: The auth flow proxies SIAKAD login and binds it to GitHub OAuth, issuing JWT + refresh tokens. GitHub tokens are encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM).

tour.title: Aggregator Engine (GitHub Webhooks)
tour.description: The aggregator receives GitHub webhooks (push/PR/release), parses events, awards points with anti-gaming caps, and writes to the activity log feed.

tour.title: Showcase, Forum & Leaderboard Services
tour.description: Service layer for curating showcase repos, running the discussion forum (threads/comments/notifications), and computing the Redis-cached points leaderboard.

tour.title: Data Access & Schema
tour.description: Repositories use pgx against PostgreSQL; sqlc query files define type-safe queries; golang-migrate migrations create and evolve the schema.

tour.title: HTTP Middleware
tour.description: Cross-cutting middleware handles JWT auth, CORS, Redis-backed rate limiting (per-IP and per-user), and response caching.

tour.title: Frontend Shell & Auth
tour.description: The Next.js root layout mounts global providers and navigation; middleware gates protected routes; the API/auth client libs talk to the backend.

tour.title: Frontend Feature Flows
tour.description: Feature components implement the user-facing flows: activity feed, repo showcase, leaderboard, public profile, and the discussion forum.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Deployment
tour.description: Multi-stage Dockerfiles build hardened images; Docker Compose orchestrates backend, frontend, PostgreSQL, Redis, and nginx; the prod override adds security hardening.
